args.query: Etsy official help item arrived damaged seller response open case after contacting seller 48 hours

content.content:
content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How to Open a Case

  - url: https://help.etsy.com/hc/en-us/articles/5745586898199-How-to-Open-a-Case

  - source: Etsy Help

  - domain: help.etsy.com


  ## Content


  # How to Open a Case


  A buyer can open a case to ask Etsy for help with an order after contacting the
  seller and after the order becomes eligible.


  For physical items and digital items ready for immediate download, both of these
  conditions must be met before the case option is available:


  - The estimated delivery date provided for the order at the time of purchase has
  passed.

  - At least 48 hours have passed since the buyer sent the seller a **Help with order**
  request.


  If the case option is not shown, Etsy may indicate that it is too early, too late,
  or that a Help with order message still needs to be sent. When it is too early,
  the Help with order area can show the date and time when the case process becomes
  available. It can also show how long the buyer has left to open a case. For these
  orders, the case-opening period ends 30 days after the estimated delivery date.


  ## How to open a case


  1. Sign in to Etsy and go to **Your account**.

  2. Open **Purchases and reviews**.

  3. Select **Help with order** next to the relevant purchase.

  4. Choose the reason help is needed.

  5. Select a preferred refund method if Etsy offers that choice.

  6. Add the details Etsy should know about the problem.

  7. Select **Submit to Etsy**.


  An Etsy account is required. A buyer who checked out as a guest must create or use
  an Etsy account and link the order before opening a case.


  ## Case types


  Etsy lists three case types:


  - **Non-delivery:** the order was not received, or it arrived at least seven days
  after the estimated delivery window.

  - **Not as described:** the item differs materially from the listing, such as the
  wrong design, model, color, quantity, or represented condition.

  - **Item arrived damaged:** the buyer received the item, but it was damaged.


  When contacting the seller, the buyer should describe the order problem, state the
  preferred resolution, and provide useful details. Photographs may be included when
  the item is damaged or differs from the order.


  After a case is opened, Etsy works with the buyer and seller and may request further
  information through the case log. Requested information may include photographs,
  measurements, third-party evaluations, or other records needed to review the case.
  Etsy notifies both parties when it reaches a final decision.


  A case may close automatically after a full refund or if the buyer starts a separate
  dispute with a card provider or PayPal. Etsy generally does not allow an Etsy case
  and a separate payment dispute to proceed for the same transaction.

  '

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: How to Get Help with An Order

  - url: https://help.etsy.com/hc/en-us/articles/4402660818583-How-to-Get-Help-with-An-Order

  - source: Etsy Help

  - domain: help.etsy.com


  ## Content


  # How to Get Help with An Order


  The seller who received the order is normally the first person to contact about
  an order problem. Sellers can help with questions about order status, shipping-address
  changes, order changes, refunds, returns, exchanges, and cancellation requests.


  ## Send a Help with order request


  On Etsy.com:


  1. Sign in and go to **Your account**.

  2. Select **Purchases and reviews**.

  3. Find the purchase and select **Help with Order**.

  4. Choose the type of help needed.

  5. Add a message to the seller, choose a requested resolution when the option is
  available, and provide images if they are relevant.

  6. Select **Send help request**.


  In the Etsy app, open **Purchases**, choose the order, tap **Help**, select the
  order issue, enter the message and requested resolution, and submit the help request.


  The seller can usually answer questions and address an order issue. If the seller
  is unable to help, the buyer may need to open a case with Etsy for further assistance.
  Etsy states that a buyer who contacted the seller more than 48 hours ago can choose
  **Open a case** when the order meets the case requirements.


  For qualifying orders under Etsy Purchase Protection, an item may be refunded when
  it does not arrive, arrives damaged, or does not match the item description or photographs.


  A buyer who checked out as a guest must link the guest order to an Etsy account
  before opening a case. The linked account allows the buyer to use the order-help
  and case process associated with the purchase.

  '
metadata: {}
title: How to Get Help with An Order
url: https://help.etsy.com/hc/en-us/articles/4402660818583-How-to-Get-Help-with-An-Order

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Etsy''s Purchase Protection Program

  - url: https://help.etsy.com/hc/en-us/articles/7471925990807-Etsy-s-Purchase-Protection-Program

  - source: Etsy Help

  - domain: help.etsy.com


  ## Content


  # Etsy''s Purchase Protection Program


  Etsy Purchase Protection covers qualifying order problems. Depending on the order
  and Etsy''s review, a buyer may receive a refund when an item:


  - Does not arrive.

  - Arrives damaged.

  - Arrives at least seven days after the maximum estimated delivery window shown
  at checkout.

  - Differs significantly from the listing description or photographs.


  An order that does not meet the Purchase Protection criteria may still be subject
  to the seller''s own cancellation, return, or exchange policies.


  ## Requesting help


  The first step is to contact the seller by starting a Help request from the order.
  If the issue has not been resolved and 48 hours have passed since the buyer first
  messaged the seller through that process, the buyer may be able to ask Etsy to step
  in and review a refund request.


  Etsy confirms whether the order is eligible and sends a confirmation if a refund
  is approved. Opening a request or case does not by itself determine the final outcome.


  A buyer must have an Etsy account to begin the Help request and case process. A
  guest purchase must be connected to a new or existing Etsy account before the buyer
  can use Purchase Protection.


  The program excludes some circumstances. For example, problems caused by a buyer''s
  delay or refusal to pay customs or import charges do not qualify. Late delivery
  caused by events outside the seller''s control, such as severe weather, a carrier
  strike, war, or civil unrest, may also be excluded.


  Etsy states that cases are reviewed by its case team. An occasional case does not
  automatically penalize a seller or the seller''s shop.

  '

title: Etsy's Purchase Protection Program
url: https://help.etsy.com/hc/en-us/articles/7471925990807-Etsy-s-Purchase-Protection-Program

Order **ORD-74298163** is marked **delivered**, and the issue recorded is **item arrived damaged**.

Current status:
- You contacted the seller on **July 8 at 9:00 AM**.
- The seller replied on **July 9 at 12:34 PM**, but the issue was recorded as **unresolved**.
- No refund, return, warranty process, or Etsy case is currently open.
- The estimated delivery date was **July 7**, so it has passed.

**What to do next:** Open an Etsy case now. Etsy’s requirements appear satisfied: the estimated delivery date has passed, and more than 48 hours have elapsed since your Help with order request. Etsy’s official guidance says damaged-item cases can be submitted through:

**Etsy → Your account → Purchases and reviews → Help with order → select the order → Item arrived damaged → Submit to Etsy**

Include:
- Clear photos of the damage and packaging
- A concise description of what happened
- Your preferred resolution, such as a full refund
- The seller’s unresolved response, if it is not already visible in the order message history

Etsy may request additional information during its review, and eligibility for a refund is determined by Etsy. Do not start a separate card/PayPal dispute for the same transaction while pursuing an Etsy case, since Etsy generally does not allow both processes to proceed simultaneously.

Sources: [Etsy: How to Open a Case](https://help.etsy.com/hc/en-us/articles/5745586898199-How-to-Open-a-Case), [Etsy: How to Get Help with an Order](https://help.etsy.com/hc/en-us/articles/4402660818583-How-to-Get-Help-with-An-Order).

Include:

Etsy may request additional information during its review, and eligibility for a refund is determined by Etsy. Do not start a separate card/PayPal dispute for the same transaction while pursuing an Etsy case, since Etsy generally does not allow both processes to proceed simultaneously.
